title: ruellia patula var. prostrata
intro: Ruellia patula var. prostrata, also known as the creeping ruellia or blue trumpetflower, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant that belongs to the Acanthaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ruellia patula var. prostrata include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
